title: spiranthera atlantica
intro: Spiranthera atlantica, also known as the Brazilian rosewood, is a plant species native to the Brazilian Atlantic Forest, belonging to the family Rutaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The extracts of Spiranthera atlantica have been reported to possess various health benefits, such as antioxidant, anti-diabetic, and anti-cancer properties. The plant's leaves and bark are used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as being used in traditional remedies for various ailments.
